commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the compatibility-sensitive cap and normal merge-gate handling.; Cleared: The diff reduces an unbounded Discord REST response-memory risk and adds no dependency, workflow, secret, or supply-chain exposure. (sha=140a4f13cddf9f6c5aaf190083d69e6c24a4608d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces Discord RequestClient's unbounded REST success-body read with the shared response-limit helper and adds regression tests for overflow, idle timeout, and normal payload parsing.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +72. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has an unbounded `response.text()` in `RequestClient.executeRequest`, and the PR body provides before/after terminal proof against that path; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the compatibility-sensitive cap and normal merge-gate handling.

target.title: fix(discord): bound REST response body to prevent OOM flood
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Discord REST main response path read its body with an **unbounded** `await response.text()` (`extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.ts:252`) before JSON-parsing it. A controlled or hijacked endpoint — a malicious gateway redirecting REST traffic, or a compromised proxy in front of the Discord API — can stream an arbitrarily large response body that is fully buffered into memory, an unbounded-flood → OOM / DoS vector against any bot using the Discord extension. This bounds the read with the canonical `readResponseWithLimit` helper (an 8 MiB cap, far above any legitimate Discord JSON payload) plus an idle timeout, cancelling the stream at the cap or on stall instead of buffering unbounded — the symmetric fix to PR #95108, which bounded the analogous Anthropic Messages error-response read with the same helper.

## Evidence

A standalone `tsx proof.mts` drives the **real** `RequestClient.executeRequest` main path (line 252), with `vitest` regression tests and `oxlint` / `tsgo` on the changed files (worktree off `upstream/main`, repo `node_modules` symlinked):

- **Before (unbounded)**: `await response.text()` against a flood body (4 MiB/chunk) buffered the **entire 256 MiB** flood — `heapUsed` grew **+236.55 MiB**, stream `cancel` count **0** (drained everything). A real endpoint flooding indefinitely = OOM.
- **After (bounded, this PR)**: the same flood through `RequestClient.get(...)` pulled only **4 chunks**, hit the 8 MiB cap, **cancelled the stream once** (heap grew only **+16.69 MiB**), and threw `Discord REST response body exceeds 8388608 bytes (received 12582912)`. A stalled stream is cancelled with `Discord REST response stalled: no data received for 80ms`.
- **Negative control**: a normal ~100 KB JSON payload (`{id, name, topic}`) still parses fully — `id`/`name`/`topic.length` all match, no false positive (legitimate large-but-bounded responses are not broken).
- **Tests / lint / types**: `vitest extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.test.ts` → **34 passed (3 new)** — bounded overflow, idle-timeout stall, normal-payload control; `oxlint` on both changed files → exit 0; `tsgo --noEmit` on `extensions/discord/tsconfig.json` + scoped test tsconfig → exit 0.

---

## Summary

The Discord REST main response path read the body with an **unbounded** `await response.text()` (`extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.ts:252`) before JSON-parsing it. A controlled or hijacked endpoint (e.g. a malicious gateway redirecting REST traffic, a compromised proxy) can stream an arbitrarily large response body, which is fully buffered into memory — an unbounded-flood → OOM vector.

This wraps the read in the canonical `readResponseWithLimit` helper (the same one PR #95108 used to bound the Anthropic Messages error-response read), with an **8 MiB cap** — far above any legitimate Discord JSON payload (bulk `messages?limit=100` / full guild objects stay in the low hundreds of KB) — plus an **idle timeout** tied to the request timeout. The stream is cancelled at the cap or on stall instead of buffering unbounded, and normal payloads still parse fully.

## Changes

- `extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.ts`
  - Import `readResponseWithLimit` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime` (the established extension-side entry that re-exports the `@openclaw/media-core` helper from #95108 — no new abstraction).
  - Add `DISCORD_REST_RESPONSE_BODY_MAX_BYTES = 8 * 1024 * 1024` and a small `readResponseBodyText(response, idleTimeoutMs)` wrapper that caps the body, sets `chunkTimeoutMs` to the request timeout, and throws bounded `onOverflow` / `onIdleTimeout` errors.
  - Replace the line-252 `await response.text()` on the main path with the bounded reader; the result is still fed to `coerceResponseBody`, so normal JSON / 204 / 429 / error payloads parse exactly as before.
- `extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.test.ts`
  - Regression tests: bounded overflow (flood body → cap error + stream cancelled, only a few chunks pulled), idle-timeout stall (stalled stream cancelled with the stall error), and a normal-payload control that still parses.

## Real behavior proof

- **What was run**: a standalone `tsx proof.mts` driving the real `RequestClient.executeRequest` main path (line 252), plus the new `vitest` regression tests and `oxlint` / `tsgo` on the changed files. Worktree off `upstream/main` with the repo `node_modules` symlinked.
- **Before (unbounded)**: `await response.text()` against a flood body (4 MiB/chunk) buffered the **entire 256 MiB** flood into memory — `heapUsed` grew **+236.55 MiB**, `cancel` count **0** (drained everything). A real endpoint flooding indefinitely = OOM.
- **After (bounded, this PR)**: the same flood through `RequestClient.get(...)` pulled only **4 chunks**, hit the 8 MiB cap, **cancelled the stream once** (heap grew only +16.69 MiB), and threw `Discord REST response body exceeds 8388608 bytes (received 12582912)`. Idle case: a stalled stream is cancelled with `Discord REST response stalled: no data received for 80ms`.
- **Negative control**: a normal ~100 KB JSON payload (`{id, name, topic}`) still parses fully — `id`/`name`/`topic.length` all match, no false positive (proving legitimate large-but-bounded responses are not broken).
- **Readback**: the proof reads the actual thrown `Error.message`, the stream's `cancel` callback invocation count + cancel `reason`, the resolved/parsed object, and `process.memoryUsage().heapUsed` deltas across the real read path (not a mock of the helper).
- **Tests / lint / types**: `vitest extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.test.ts` → 34 passed (3 new); `oxlint` on both changed files → exit 0; `tsgo --noEmit` on `extensions/discord/tsconfig.json` and a scoped test tsconfig including the test file → exit 0.
- **Result**: all proof assertions passed (`=== 全部 proof 断言通过 ✅ ===`).

**Label**: security

## Symmetry with #95108

#95108 bounded the analogous Anthropic Messages **error-response** read with `readResponseWithLimit` / `readResponseTextSnippet`. This PR is the symmetric fix for the Discord REST **main-response** path, reusing the same `@openclaw/media-core` helper (via the `response-limit-runtime` plugin entry) rather than introducing a new abstraction. (Note: `extensions/discord/src/api.ts:194` has the same unbounded `res.text()` on its main path and could be a follow-up.)

---

AI-assisted: implemented and verified with AI assistance; all proof commands were run for real against the actual code path.
